title: Bleasdalea vitiensis
taxonomy: Bleasdalea vitiensis is classified as a flowering plant belonging to the family Ranunculaceae. This plant is a member of the genus Bleasdalea and is native to the Pacific Islands. The classification of Bleasdalea vitiensis is further defined by its specific characteristics and genetic makeup. As a member of the Ranunculaceae family, Bleasdalea vitiensis shares similarities with other plants in this family, such as the buttercup and the clematis. Through its classification, scientists can better understand the plant's evolutionary history and its place within the plant kingdom.
